title: Sarthak and his Set of Primes

content.problemStatement:
Sarthak has a set SSS of NNN distinct prime numbers. He grew very fond of that set, and now he only likes a positive integer XXX if all of its prime factors belong to SSS. Given the set and a positive integer MMM, can you find out how many integers he likes which are not more than MMM?

**Note:** Sarthak always like the number 111.


Input Format

The first line of each input contains TTT - the number of test cases. The test cases then follow.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM - the size of SSS and the limit defined in the statement.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers S1,S2,…,SNS_1, S_2, \dots, S_NS1​,S2​,…,SN​ - the elements of SSS.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a single line the number of positive integers Sarthak likes that is not more than MMM.
constraints: SSS has NNN distinct prime numbers

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Sarthak likes the numbers 111, 333, 555 and 999.
